options.label: A
options.text: Electricity that flows through wires in a circuit

options.label: B
options.text: Electric charge that builds up and stays in one place

options.label: D
options.text: The movement of electrons through a conductor

correct_answer: Electric charge that builds up and stays in one place
explanation: Static electricity is electric charge that accumulates on objects and remains stationary. This differs from current electricity, which flows through circuits. It builds up when electrons transfer between materials through friction or contact.
